decks.notes: This deck focuses on utilizing Amulet of Vigor to accelerate mana production and enable explosive plays with Primeval Titan. The goal is to set up a loop of bouncing lands with cards like Gruul Turf and Simic Growth Chamber to generate immense amounts of mana, then use cards like Scapeshift to search for and play multiple lands in a single turn, eventually winning the game with a massive Primeval Titan or a Malevolent Rumble. The inclusion of Urza's Saga adds versatility and additional ways to generate value in the deck's combo strategy.

Utilizing artifacts and sacrificing them for value is the main focus of this deck. With cards like Engineered Explosives and Claws of Gix, the deck aims to control the board while also developing its own game plan. Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Krang, Master Mind are key in recurring crucial artifacts from the graveyard. The deck is also equipped with countermagic in Metallic Rebuke to protect its threats. Urza's Saga is a key land that helps find important artifacts to further strengthen the board presence. Overall, the deck is built to be proactive and resilient in the face of opposition.

This deck focuses on utilizing disruptive cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the opponent's board while also applying pressure with aggressive creatures like Overlord of the Balemurk and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd. Quantum Riddler helps to manipulate the opponent's deck, while cards like Solitude provide removal options. Flickerwisp and Ephemerate offer tempo plays and synergize well with creatures like Witch Enchanter and Emperor of Bones. Teferi, Time Raveler provides additional disruption, while the mana base allows for flexibility in casting spells and activating abilities.

This deck focuses on generating a lot of mana quickly through spells like Birthing Ritual and Shardless Agent to play powerful creatures such as Subtlety and Ice-Fang Coatl. The deck also includes counterspells like Flare of Denial and Force of Negation to protect your creatures and disrupt your opponent's game plan. With efficient creatures and disruption spells, this deck aims to apply constant pressure on the opponent and control the game tempo. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Fblthp, the Lost and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student for card advantage and selection.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts and synergies between them to quickly put pressure on the opponent. It aims to flood the board with artifact creatures and utilize Engineered Explosives and Metallic Rebuke to control the game. The strategy also includes using Urza's Saga to tutor for key pieces and generate value over time. Emry, Lurker of the Loch is used to recur artifacts from the graveyard, while Arcbound Ravager can be sacrificed to increase the power of other creatures. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Tormod's Crypt and Claws of Gix to disrupt the opponent's strategy.

The main idea of this Dimir Control deck is to manipulate the opponent's hand and board state while controlling the game with various utility spells and creatures. By using cards like Subtlety, Counterspell, and Fatal Push, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy and remove threats. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Quantum Riddler provide card advantage and selection, while cards like Thoughtseize and Sink into Stupor disrupt the opponent's hand. Ultimately, the deck looks to out-value the opponent with efficient removal and powerful control elements.

The deck focuses on quickly filling up the graveyard with powerful creatures and cycling cards, using cards like Endurance and Street Wraith to put creatures into the graveyard. The goal is to cast Living End to bring back all the creatures from the graveyard, overwhelming the opponent with a huge board presence. Cards like Generous Ent and Shardless Agent help put creatures into the graveyard, while cards like Subtlety and Wistfulness can disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck also includes protection spells like Force of Negation to ensure the Living End combo goes off successfully.

The deck focuses on quickly generating mana through rituals and medallions to cast multiple spells in a single turn. Ral's ability to copy spells, along with Glimpse the Impossible, allows for explosive turns to overwhelm the opponent. The inclusion of Wish adds flexibility to search for specific cards needed to combo off. The deck is designed to win the game quickly through a big Grapeshot finish or by chaining spells with Past in Flames for a lethal attack. Overall, the strategy revolves around efficient mana generation and spell casting to execute a powerful combo.

The deck focuses on utilizing land ramp and combos to quickly ramp up to play Primeval Titan and other utility creatures. By using cards like Amulet of Vigor and Arboreal Grazer, the deck can get ahead on mana and take advantage of powerful land synergies. The goal is to set up a board state where the player can use Primeval Titan to fetch key lands like Urza's Saga and use them to generate powerful effects and overwhelming advantage. The deck combines elements of ramp, combo, and utility lands to create a versatile and powerful strategy.

The deck focuses on utilizing the interaction between Amulet of Vigor and bounce lands like Simic Growth Chamber to generate a large amount of mana quickly. This mana is then used to cast Primeval Titan and search for powerful lands like Urza's Saga, Crumbling Vestige, and Boseiju, Who Endures. The deck also includes cards like Scapeshift and Malevolent Rumble as win conditions to quickly close out games. By ramping quickly and efficiently, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with powerful threats and combo off for a fast victory.

The deck focuses on creating a fast and aggressive strategy that aims to overwhelm the opponent with early creatures and burn spells. It includes powerful creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Seasoned Pyromancer, as well as burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Thraben Charm. The deck also includes cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Guide of Souls to provide additional support and versatility. With a mix of creatures, burn spells, and disruption, the Boros Aggro deck is designed to apply constant pressure and close out games quickly.

The deck focuses on quickly deploying artifacts and creatures to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive plays. Emry, Tamiyo, and Quantum Riddler help to generate card advantage and provide versatility in the deck's strategy. The mix of efficient removal and counter spells ensures that the opponent is constantly on the back foot. By utilizing mana acceleration from Mox Opal and Mox Amber, as well as sacrificing artifacts for value, the deck aims to finish the game quickly before the opponent can stabilize. The synergy between artifact creatures and spells creates a powerful and versatile aggro strategy.

This deck aims to disrupt opponents with tax effects while quickly deploying aggressive threats to win the game. It combines powerful creatures like Phelia and Ragavan with removal spells like Solitude and Galvanic Discharge to control the board. Quantum Riddler adds card advantage and consistency to the deck. Consign to Memory and Ephemerate provide additional value by recycling creatures and gaining tempo advantage. The deck also includes versatile removal options like Prismatic Ending and Wrath of the Skies to deal with a variety of threats. Teferi, Time Raveler helps protect the game plan and enable powerful plays.

The deck focuses on generating mana and casting spells quickly to create a storm of spells and trigger multiple synergies, eventually leading to a big finishing combo like Grapeshot. It includes cards like Ral, Monsoon Mage and Ruby Medallion to reduce the cost of spells, rituals like Desperate Ritual and Pyretic Ritual to generate mana, and recursion spells like Past in Flames to reuse spells from the graveyard. The deck also includes cards like Wish to search for specific combo pieces and cards like Reckless Impulse for card advantage.

This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with small, aggressive creatures and cheap, efficient spells. By using cards like Dragon's Rage Channeler, Monastery Swiftspear, and Slickshot Show-Off, alongside burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart, the deck aims to deal damage early and often. Mishra's Bauble and Preordain help with card selection and drawing into the right cards, while Expressive Iteration provides card advantage. The combination of creatures, burn spells, and card draw allows the deck to maintain pressure and close out games quickly.

The deck focuses on assembling a variety of creatures that can interact with each other to create powerful synergies and combos. It includes toolbox creatures like Badgermole Cub and Yawgmoth, Thran Physician, along with support cards like Agatha's Soul Cauldron and Malevolent Rumble. The deck utilizes cards like Chord of Calling and Green Sun's Zenith to search for specific creatures when needed. The goal is to create a resilient board presence and disrupt the opponent's strategy while setting up game-winning combos.
